role: The veto gate. Sits last but can block a financially attractive deal. Not a substitute for binding legal advice.

framework_selection.profile: B2B SaaS, EU personal data, customer-facing AI, enterprise procurement, US + EU market

applicable.status: informal program — no Art. 30 records, no DPIA on AI
unassessed — AI on EU personal data, no risk classification
applicable.trigger: AI system deployed on EU personal data

applicable.status: held WITH one exception (access-review cycle skipped)
not held — buyer-expected for enterprise upmarket

framework_selection.dependency_note: GDPR + EU AI Act are the binding pair; SOC 2 <-> ISO 27001 share ~75% control overlap (evidence reuse anchor).

mock_audit.method: audit_simulator — finding scenarios with IIA severity distribution (<=15% critical/major healthy)
mock_audit.severity_health: UNHEALTHY for sign-as-is — 22% critical (2/9) exceeds the <=15% healthy ceiling; concentrated in EU data + AI

critical_findings.finding: Two EU subprocessors operating with no DPA
AI feature trains/infers on EU personal data with no lawful basis, no DPIA, no EU AI Act risk classification

major_findings.finding: Access-review control skipped a cycle -> report exception; consistent operation not demonstrated
All EU data in us-east-1, no residency option

high_leverage_artifacts: AI system inventory + DPIA (EU AI Act + GDPR)
evidence_pool.reuse_note: Fixing the DPA + access-review gaps satisfies controls across SOC 2, ISO 27001 and GDPR simultaneously.

verdict: CONDITIONAL — STAGE-2 CANDIDATE (this gate CAN return BLOCK; here it gates the deal on binding pre-close conditions rather than vetoing outright)
would_be_block_if: Either critical finding (missing DPAs OR ungoverned AI on EU personal data) is unresolved at close, OR the seller will not warrant remediation -> BLOCK, regardless of how attractive the price is.
binding_conditions_to_close: DPAs executed with both EU subprocessors BEFORE EU data continues to flow (clears F-01)
EU AI Act risk classification + DPIA on the AI feature completed and reviewed by EU counsel (clears F-02)
SOC 2 access-review exception remediated and bridge letter obtained (clears F-03)
interaction_with_valuation: Valuation found a workable price ($252M), but this gate makes that offer contingent: the conditions are reps/warranties + conditions precedent in the SPA, and the AI/DPA exposure is escrow-backed. Price does not buy past the gate.
top_3_actions: Engage EU privacy + AI-product counsel this week (pre-exclusivity-close)
Make the four binding conditions reps/warranties with an indemnity escrow
Re-run audit_simulator post-remediation to confirm severity distribution is healthy before funding
source: compliance-os (framework_selector + audit_simulator severity distribution + evidence-pool reuse) + compliance-readiness (six forcing questions, READY/STAGE-2/NOT-READY verdict) + soc2-audit-prep (observation-period exception logic)
